pre_execution_gate.instruction: Before writing ANY code, read all attached files, then produce a summary of your understanding in the chat. The summary MUST include: 1. Task scope (what you will benchmark and why) 2. Variable under test (GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION — NOT the same as sparse attention) 3. Test matrix dimensions (2 XAttention configs × 4 bands × 7 runs) 4. Fixed constants (NAT=3, Draft-A, sparse_attention=OFF, do_sample=False) 5. Evidence artifact path 6. Quality gate criteria 7. Governance updates required 8. Pre-condition verification checklist Wait for Lead Architect approval before proceeding.

pre_conditions.check: Branch: `feature/p5-task4-4-xattention-sweep` created from merged main HEAD (after merge of `feature/p5-task4-3b-sparse-attention`). Verify HEAD contains Task 4.3 and 4.3b evidence artifacts and governance updates.

Task 4.3 evidence artifact exists: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_3_nat_sweep_matrix.json` Parse it, extract NAT=3 entries for bands [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384]. These are REFERENCE ONLY for calibration check — NOT used as baselines. Task 4.4 runs BOTH XAttention OFF and ON fresh.

Verify GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION is a valid pipeline construction property. Test: create a minimal pipeline with `LLMPipeline(path, "GPU", GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION=True)` and verify no exception on construction. If the property is not recognized or causes an error, ABORT with `PROPERTY_NOT_AVAILABLE`.
Python .venv active with openvino_genai, psutil, transformers.
Test suite baseline: run `python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header` and record pass count. No test failures expected. Task 4.4 adds NO unit tests (benchmark harness only).

scope.summary: Sweep GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION {OFF, ON} across 4 context bands [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384] with all other parameters fixed (NAT=3, Draft-A, sparse_attention=OFF, do_sample=False). Determine whether the P5-005b finding (XAttention OFF is better at 4K) holds at longer context, or whether XAttention ON becomes beneficial where speculative decoding is weak or inert.
scope.rationale: P5-005b found GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION OFF produced higher TPS at 4K (10.02 vs 9.74 tps — 2.9% delta). That test was conducted at a single context band. Task 4.3 revealed that speculative decoding acceptance rate collapses to 0.000 at ≥16K, meaning the pipeline becomes purely autoregressive at long context. The SDPA optimization accelerates attention computation — its benefit may emerge precisely where speculative decoding can no longer help. This sweep tests whether XAttention's effect is context-length-dependent and whether the P5-005b finding is a universal constant or a short-context artifact.
scope.naming_disambiguation:
GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION is the **GPU plugin SDPA kernel optimization**. It controls whether the OpenVINO GPU plugin uses Scaled Dot-Product Attention kernel fusion. It is a pipeline construction property, NOT a SchedulerConfig field.
This is COMPLETELY SEPARATE from: - `SchedulerConfig.use_sparse_attention` (scheduler KV cache eviction — tested in Task 4.3b, DEFERRED) - `SparseAttentionMode.TRISHAPE` / `SparseAttentionMode.XATTENTION` (scheduler eviction patterns)
The "XAttention" name in Task 4.4 title refers to the GPU SDPA optimization feature, which Intel sometimes markets as "XAttention" in their documentation. Do NOT confuse this with SparseAttentionMode.XATTENTION from Task 4.3b.
scope.harness_declaration: NO_FULL_HARNESS — new script, no prior XAttention × multi-band benchmark. Pattern derived from run_p5_task4_3b_sparse_attention.py (list-input generate, RSS sampler, acceptance rate extraction, atomic JSON writes, AC power enforcement).
scope.no_production_code_changes: Task 4.4 touches phase2_gates/ and docs/ ONLY. No modifications to services/, shared/, launcher/, or tests/.

dimension.name: xattention
dimension.note: OFF: GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION not set (or explicitly False) — default behavior. ON: GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION=True passed to LLMPipeline constructor. Each value requires a SEPARATE pipeline compilation (GPU plugin config is compile-time). NAMING: "XAttention" here means GPU SDPA kernel, NOT SparseAttentionMode.XATTENTION.

dimension.name: prompt_band
dimension.note: 4096: AO baseline reference band. Speculative decoding effective (AR ~0.457). 8192: AO/Code Agent primary production band. Spec-decode fading (AR ~0.378). 12288: Regime change boundary. Spec-decode weak (AR ~0.378 but TPS drops 38%). 16384: AR=0.000 collapse. Spec-decode inert — purely autoregressive. 20480 excluded: locked out by DEC-03 (context cap = 16,384 tokens).

test_matrix.execution_order:
1. Compile Pipeline A: XAttention OFF (no GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION property) 2. Run Pipeline A at [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384] (band-ascending) 3. Calibration check: compare Pipeline A 4K TPS with Task 4.3 reference (8.065 tps) 4. Save intermediate results 5. Delete Pipeline A, gc.collect() 6. Compile Pipeline B: XAttention ON (GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION=True) 7. Run Pipeline B at [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384] (band-ascending) 8. Save intermediate results 9. Compute deltas and quality gates 10. Write final evidence artifact
XAttention OFF runs FIRST because it is the current production default (PROVISIONAL BEST). If Pipeline B compilation fails (SDPA not supported), disposition is XATTENTION_NOT_AVAILABLE and Pipeline A data validates the OFF default.

pipeline_configs.config: scheduler = SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 # sparse_attention=OFF — fixed constant per Task 4.3b SPARSE_DEFERRED pipeline = LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), ) # Note: GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION is NOT set — defaults to OFF. # This is functionally identical to Task 4.3 pipeline construction.

scheduler = SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 pipeline = LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), **{"GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION": True}, ) # Note: if GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION is passed as a string property, # the correct syntax may be: # LLMPipeline(..., config={"GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION": "YES"}) # or via ov.Core().set_property("GPU", {"GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION": True}) # # VERIFY THE EXACT API during PC-04. The P5-005b harness used one of these patterns. # If unsure, check the OpenVINO GenAI 2026.0 LLMPipeline constructor signature. # The property MUST be applied at pipeline compile time, not per-generate.
pipeline_configs.config_verification: After compiling Pipeline B, verify the property took effect. If OpenVINO logs a warning like "Unknown property GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION" or silently ignores it, the test is invalid. Check: 1. Pipeline B compile time differs from Pipeline A (SDPA optimization changes compilation strategy — non-trivial delta expected). 2. Pipeline B 4K TPS differs from Pipeline A 4K TPS by any non-zero amount (even if small). Identical TPS at 4K across 5 runs strongly suggests the property was ignored. If both checks suggest the property was ignored: ABORT and report `PROPERTY_IGNORED` disposition.

test_matrix.generation_config: # Fixed for ALL configs: cfg = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() cfg.max_new_tokens = 128 cfg.do_sample = False cfg.temperature = 0.0 cfg.top_k = 1 cfg.top_p = 1.0 cfg.num_assistant_tokens = 3 # NAT=3 — LOCKED (DEC-01) cfg.assistant_confidence_threshold = 0.0
test_matrix.prompt_construction: Use IDENTICAL prompt construction to Tasks 4.3 and 4.3b: - Tokenizer: AutoTokenizer from Qwen3-14B model directory. - System prompt: "You are a helpful assistant." - User content: padded to target token count per band. - Chat template: apply_chat_template with add_generation_prompt=True. - Build prompt ONCE per band, reuse across XAttention settings. Prompts MUST be byte-identical to Task 4.3/4.3b prompts at matching bands.

per_run.field: Wall-clock tokens/second for full target+draft pipeline. Source: extended_perf_metrics.get_throughput().mean — PRIMARY METRIC FOR THIS TASK.
Mean draft model forward pass time. Source: extended_perf_metrics.draft_model_metrics.get_inference_duration().mean
NAT × total_speculative_episodes. Source: raw_metrics.m_batch_sizes count × 3 (NAT=3 fixed)
sum(b - 1 for b in m_batch_sizes). Source: raw_metrics.m_batch_sizes
Per-step array [step_1_rate, step_2_rate, step_3_rate]. Source: m_batch_sizes analysis (3 steps since NAT=3)
Process RSS high-water mark. Source: RssSampler thread (0.01s polling interval)

supplementary.field: Time to compile Pipeline A (XAttention OFF).
Time to compile Pipeline B (XAttention ON).
Pipeline A 4K TPS for cross-session validation.

mandatory_measurements.validation_rule: A run missing ANY of the 7 fields is INVALID and must not be counted. If valid_count drops below 3 for any config, mark as INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

api_critical_patterns.pattern: pipeline.generate([prompt], gen_config, streamer_callback) NEVER: pipeline.generate(prompt, gen_config, streamer_callback) Bare str input returns bare str with NO perf_metrics, NO extended_perf_metrics. List input returns DecodedResults with .texts[0], .perf_metrics, .extended_perf_metrics.

output = pipeline.generate([prompt], gc, cb) epm = output.extended_perf_metrics epm.get_throughput().mean # native combined TPS epm.get_ttft().mean # native TTFT (ms) epm.draft_model_metrics.get_inference_duration().mean # draft forward ms epm.get_num_accepted_tokens() # integer accepted count

raw = output.perf_metrics.raw_metrics batch_sizes = list(raw.m_batch_sizes) # batch_sizes[i] = accepted_tokens + 1 for speculative episode i # Aggregate: sum(b-1 for b in batch_sizes) / (3 × len(batch_sizes))

calibration_check.instruction: After Pipeline A completes the 4K band, compare its mean TPS with the Task 4.3 NAT=3 reference value (8.065 tps). This validates cross-session consistency. If within ±15%: proceed (thermal/session variance is acceptable). If outside ±15%: flag as CALIBRATION_WARNING in evidence but proceed anyway. This is a defensive sanity check, NOT a quality gate blocker.

reference_data.note: Task 4.3 NAT=3 per-band (informational — for calibration only)

quality_gate.gate: All 7 mandatory fields present for all measured runs at all 4 bands. Threshold: zero invalid runs across all 8 configs.
valid_count >= 5 for all configurations. If any config has valid_count < 5, disposition = INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.

For each band, compute: tps_delta_pct = (tps_on - tps_off) / tps_off × 100 Classification per band: tps_delta_pct > +3%: ON_WINS — XAttention improves throughput. tps_delta_pct in [-3%, +3%]: EQUIVALENT — within noise floor. tps_delta_pct < -3%: OFF_WINS — XAttention hurts throughput. If EQUIVALENT at all bands: P5-005b finding CONFIRMED (effectively no difference at production precision). Lock OFF as default (simpler, no additional GPU compilation). If ON_WINS at any band: context-dependent result — requires profiling. If OFF_WINS at all bands: P5-005b finding CONFIRMED emphatically. Lock OFF globally.

For each band, compute: ttft_delta_pct = (ttft_off - ttft_on) / ttft_off × 100 Positive = ON is faster prefill. Report per-band but this is secondary to TPS. If ttft_delta_pct > 10% at any band ≥ 8192: flag SDPA_PREFILL_BENEFIT.

For each band, compute: ar_delta = ar_on - ar_off Expected: ar_delta ≈ 0 (GPU kernel optimization should not affect speculative acceptance). If |ar_delta| > 0.05 at any band: flag AR_INTERACTION — unexpected behavior. If ar_on > 0 at 16K (where ar_off = 0.000): flag AR_RECOVERY — highly unexpected.
For each band, compare: rss_delta = rss_on - rss_off Expected: near zero (SDPA optimization changes compute kernels, not memory allocation). If |rss_delta| > 500 MB at any band: flag RSS_ANOMALY.
peak_rss_mb at band 16384 for BOTH settings must be < 15,507 MB. If exceeded: MEMORY_BUDGET_EXCEEDED.

Compare pipeline compilation times: Pipeline B (ON) vs Pipeline A (OFF). Report compile_delta_ms and compile_delta_pct. If Pipeline B compile > 2× Pipeline A: flag COMPILE_OVERHEAD. One pipeline compilation per system boot — this is informational, not a blocker.
quality_gate.disposition_rules:
G-01/G-02 FAIL → INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE (cannot draw conclusions)
G-03 ALL_EQUIVALENT or ALL_OFF_WINS: → disposition = XATTENTION_OFF_LOCKED → Update ADR-012 §2.2: GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION → LOCKED (OFF). → P5-005b finding confirmed across context range.
G-03 MIXED (ON wins at some bands, OFF/EQUIVALENT at others): → disposition = CONTEXT_DEPENDENT → Update ADR-012 §2.2: GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION → CONTEXT_DEPENDENT. → Document per-band optimal setting in workload profiles. → If ON wins ONLY at 16K (where AR=0.000): note that this benefit is only relevant for USE-CASE-005 Code Agent at long context. → PA always uses 4K band maximum. AO uses up to 12K. Only Code Agent reaches 16K.
G-03 ALL_ON_WINS: → disposition = XATTENTION_ON_LOCKED → Update ADR-012 §2.2: GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION → LOCKED (ON). → Supersedes P5-005b finding.
PROPERTY_IGNORED (from pipeline_configs.config_verification): → disposition = PROPERTY_IGNORED → Lock OFF by default (property has no measurable effect).
PROPERTY_NOT_AVAILABLE (from PC-04): → disposition = XATTENTION_NOT_AVAILABLE → Lock OFF by default (property not supported in this OpenVINO version).

governance_updates.update: Update ADR-012 §2.2 — change GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION row: | `GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION` | OFF | **LOCKED** | Task 4.4: OFF confirmed across 4K-16K context range. P5-005b finding validated at extended context bands. No throughput benefit from SDPA kernel fusion with speculative decoding pipeline. Evidence: p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json. |

Update ADR-012 §2.2 — change GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION row: | `GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION` | ON | **LOCKED** | Task 4.4: ON wins at all bands. Supersedes P5-005b finding (which tested 4K only). [summary of per-band deltas]. Evidence: p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json. |

Update ADR-012 §2.2 — change GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION row: | `GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION` | Context-dependent | **LOCKED** | Task 4.4: [per-band summary]. PA/AO (≤12K) use OFF. Code Agent (≤16K) uses ON if ON wins at 16K. Evidence: p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json. | NOTE: Context-dependent configuration requires per-component pipeline construction — this means the unified pipeline must be split. Document the architectural implication.

Update ADR-012 §2.2 — change GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION row: | `GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION` | OFF (default) | **LOCKED** | Task 4.4: [PROPERTY_IGNORED|NOT_AVAILABLE] — property has no measurable effect or is not supported in OV GenAI 2026.0. Lock OFF as the null default. Evidence: p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json. |

Update ADR-012 §2.2 — keep GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION row as EVALUATING: | `GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION` | OFF | **EVALUATING** | Task 4.4: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — [reason]. Requires retry or manual investigation. |

Append new LEDGER entry to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. Use the NEXT available entry number (count existing ### Entry N headers + 1). Format:
### Entry [N] — Task 4.4: XAttention (GPU SDPA) Independent Sweep
**Date:** 2026-03-0X (actual execution date) **Branch:** `feature/p5-task4-4-xattention-sweep` **Commit:** `[HEAD after commit]` **Type:** Empirical Evidence Collection (Configuration Optimization) **Scope:** GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION {OFF, ON} × [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384] with NAT=3 LOCKED, Draft-A, sparse_attention=OFF. 8 configs, 5 measured runs each.
Key findings: - [TPS comparison per band] - [TTFT comparison per band] - [AR interaction status] - [RSS comparison] - [Compile time difference]
Quality gate: [G-01..G-08 results] Disposition: [XATTENTION_OFF_LOCKED | ... ]
Evidence: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json`

execution_prompt.intermediate_save_strategy: Save partial results after EACH band completes for EACH pipeline. Use `p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json.partial` for intermediate saves. After all bands complete for both pipelines, rename to final artifact path. Save Pipeline A results before starting Pipeline B (crash recovery).
execution_prompt.commit_template:
feat(benchmark): Task 4.4 — XAttention (GPU SDPA) sweep across context bands
Sweep GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION {OFF, ON} at bands [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384] with NAT=3 LOCKED, Draft-A, sparse_attention=OFF. 8 configs, 5 measured runs each.
- Pipeline A (OFF): current production default - Pipeline B (ON): SDPA kernel fusion enabled - TPS primary metric; TTFT, AR, RSS secondary - Calibration: Pipeline A 4K TPS vs Task 4.3 reference (8.065 tps) - Disposition: [XATTENTION_OFF_LOCKED | XATTENTION_ON_LOCKED | CONTEXT_DEPENDENT | ...] - ADR-012 §2.2 updated: GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION → [LOCKED status] - LEDGER Entry [N]
Evidence: phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_4_xattention_sweep.json

baseline.name: P5-005b XAttention comparison (4K only, prior study)

reference_data.baseline: XAttention OFF: 10.02 tps (4K, NAT=5 in P5-005b — different NAT than production) XAttention ON: 9.74 tps (4K, NAT=5 in P5-005b — different NAT than production) Delta: -2.8% (ON is slightly worse at 4K) NOTE: P5-005b used NAT=5. Production uses NAT=3 (LOCKED). Task 4.4 uses NAT=3. The absolute TPS values will differ — the DIRECTION of the delta is what matters.

baseline.name: Task 4.3 NAT=3 per-band (cross-session reference, NOT baseline)

reference_data.baseline: Band 4096: tps=8.065, ar=0.457, ttft=11,248ms, peak_rss=6,372 MB Band 8192: tps=5.543, ar=0.378, ttft=28,869ms, peak_rss=5,707 MB Band 12288: tps=2.482, ar=0.378, ttft=100,776ms, peak_rss=3,569 MB Band 16384: tps=2.886, ar=0.000, ttft=104,875ms, peak_rss=3,562 MB

baseline.name: Task 4.3b TRISHAPE reference (informational — sparse is DEFERRED)

reference_data.baseline: TRISHAPE AR=0.000 at all bands including 4K. Not applicable to Task 4.4. sparse_attention=OFF is a fixed constant for Task 4.4.
reference_data.note: Task 4.4 runs BOTH XAttention OFF and ON from scratch in the same session. Cross-session variance is eliminated. The Task 4.3 data is reference-only for the calibration sanity check. All deltas are computed within-session (Pipeline A vs B).

execution_prompt.runtime_estimate: Pipeline A compilation (XAttention OFF): ~25-30 seconds Pipeline B compilation (XAttention ON): ~25-30 seconds (may differ with SDPA) Generate calls per pipeline: 28 (4 bands × 7 runs per band) 4096 band: 7 calls × ~12 sec = ~1.5 min 8192 band: 7 calls × ~18 sec = ~2 min 12288 band: 7 calls × ~25 sec = ~3 min 16384 band: 7 calls × ~32 sec = ~4 min Subtotal per pipeline: ~11 minutes Total for both pipelines: ~22 minutes Total with compilation + calibration + analysis: ~30-40 minutes Ceiling estimate (with OV recompile overhead): ~45 minutes Significantly shorter than Task 4.3b (no 20K band, fewer modes).
execution_prompt.attachment_order: 1. .github/copilot-instructions.md (governance — MUST) 2. docs/Task4.4_v1.xml (this prompt — PRIMARY) 3. docs/P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md (spec reference — §Task 4.4) 4. docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md (parameter table)

verification_commands.command: python -c "print([l.strip() for l in open('docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md') if 'GPU_ENABLE_SDPA' in l])"
